content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


September 20, 2002


Thomas W. Hiltachk
Bell, McAndrews, Hiltachk
& Davidian, LLP
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 801
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-02-233

Dear Mr. Hiltachk:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the California
Republican Party ("CRP") regarding the member communication provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Does Commission regulation 18531.7, relating to member communications, apply
to state political party committees?

CONCLUSION

Currently, regulation 18531.7 is withdrawn by the Commission pending
reconsideration of the regulation at its October 2002 meeting. It is anticipated, however,
that any revisions that may occur at that time will not alter the Commission's prior
determination that the regulation does not govern conduct by the California Republican
Party.


___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-02-233
Page No. 2

FACTS

You request verification on the application of newly-enacted regulation 18531.7,
"member communications" on behalf of the California Republican Party.

ANALYSIS

On August 9, 2002, the Commission adopted regulation 18531.7, pertaining to
member communications. Subdivision (a)(1), which defines the term "organization,"
provides:

"(1) An 'organization,' other than a political party, means a sole
proprietorship, firm, partnership, joint venture, syndicate, business
trust, company, corporation, limited liability company, association,
labor union, and any other organization or group of persons acting in
concert, including a committee as defined by Government Code
section 82013, but excluding a candidate or individual."

As in the noticed version of the regulation, which contained language slightly
different from the adopted language above, this section of the regulation defines
"organization" in a manner that excludes a political party from the ambit of the
regulation. Accordingly, the California Republican Party is not affected by this
regulation.

As you may be aware, regulation 18531.7 is the subject of a petition for
reconsideration. To facilitate that review, the Commission withdrew regulation 18531.7
and it therefore is not currently in effect. It is anticipated, however, that the Commission
will adopt another version of regulation 18531.7 at its next meeting in October. It is not
anticipated that the Commission will alter its prior determinations with respect to the
issue you raise.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: C. Scott Topher
Counsel, Legal Division
